title: carex flacca subsp. claviformis
intro: Carex flacca subsp. claviformis, also known as the rock sedge or rough sedge, is a subspecies of grass-like plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex flacca subsp. claviformis include its potential to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to support overall health and well-being.
